title: Exposure to the Chinese famine in early life and the risk of anaemia in adulthood.
ehr_evidence: The data are based on a subsample from Jiangsu province... from the 2002 Chinese National Nutrition and Health Survey.
summary: This study investigated the association between early life exposure to the Chinese famine (1959–1961) and the risk of anaemia in adulthood using data from a national nutrition and health survey.
key_findings: Fetal exposure to the Chinese famine was associated with a 37% increased risk of anaemia in adulthood (RR 1.37).
The association was significantly stronger among men (RR 1.87), overweight or obese individuals (RR 1.75), and those with lower educational levels (RR 2.07).
captured_features: famine exposure timing (fetal, early-childhood, mid-childhood, late-childhood)
population: 2007 adults born between 1954 and 1964 in Jiangsu province, China
limitations: Potential recall bias regarding famine exposure
